data.title: Bitcoin's Negative Correlation with Global Easing Breadth Index Observed
data.description: Bitcoin has recently shown a significant negative correlation with the Global Easing Breadth Index, which monitors the monetary policy direction of 41 central banks. According to NS3.AI, spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced their largest single-day net inflow since late February this week. Analysts suggest that institutions might be taking advantage of the current price range to accumulate Bitcoin.

data.title: WTI Crude Oil Surges Amid Middle East Tensions, Crypto Sentiment Remains Weak
data.description: Wintermute reported that geopolitical developments in the Middle East have significantly influenced global markets this week, leading to an 11% increase in WTI crude oil prices, which rose above $111. According to NS3.AI, this surge followed U.S. President Donald Trump's firm stance on the situation. Meanwhile, the cryptocurrency market continues to exhibit weak sentiment, as indicated by the Fear & Greed Index, which remains at a low level of 9. Additionally, March saw a shift in ETF flows, transitioning from a $1.32 billion net inflow to net outflows by the end of the month.

data.title: Cardano and Draper Dragon jointly established an $80 million Orion fund, focusing on RWA and institutional-grade DeFi.
data.description: According to the Cardano Foundation's official website, Cardano and venture capital firm Draper Dragon announced the launch of the Orion Fund (Draper Dragon Ecosystem Fund), with a target size of $80 million. The fund will invest in Cardano's native and integrated projects, focusing on Real-World Assets (RWA) and institutional-grade DeFi to enhance Cardano's TVL and on-chain activity. The fund is managed by Draper Dragon, with the Cardano Foundation providing ecosystem and technical support as the constitutional manager. A portion of the proceeds will be channeled back to the Cardano treasury through the ownerless entity Arouet Holdings. Draper University will serve as an acceleration partner, providing startup acceleration and talent development. The fund will prioritize projects that leverage the UTXO model to bridge Bitcoin liquidity and Cardano functionality, and will enhance transparency and community oversight through public KPI dashboards and quarterly ecosystem roundtables.

data.title: Iran Considers Options for Reopening Stock Market Amid Tensions
data.description: The U.S. military has targeted military sites on Kharg Island, according to a U.S. official, as Iran evaluates four potential strategies to reopen its stock market. According to NS3.AI, the head of the Iranian Securities and Exchange Organization outlined options ranging from maintaining fund trading under current conditions to halting all trading if hostilities intensify. Meanwhile, Argus Media data indicates that Urals crude at Port of Primorsk reached $116.05 per barrel on April 2, nearly double the $59 average projected in Russia's budget for this year. The Iranian stock market has been suspended since March 1.

data.title: JUST Platform Shows Steady Growth in TRON DeFi Ecosystem
data.description:
The JUST platform, a key component of the TRON DeFi ecosystem, has demonstrated stable performance this week. According to ChainCatcher, the platform's total value locked reached $11.01 billion, accounting for 41.96% of TRON DeFi's overall volume. The supply on the platform stands at $3.59 billion, with borrowing amounts totaling $210.28 million. Additionally, the JST token buyback and burn mechanism is progressing steadily, with over 1.08 billion tokens destroyed, representing 10.96% of the total supply, thereby reinforcing the token's value through deflationary effects.

The robust locked value data and ongoing buyback and burn activities provide strong support for JUST's long-term development. Moving forward, JUST plans to focus on product iteration and ecosystem construction to enhance capital efficiency and user experience, contributing to the healthy growth of the TRON DeFi ecosystem.

data.title: Global payment platform Paysafe launches "Pay with Crypto" encrypted payment solution
data.description: Global payments platform Paysafe announced the launch of its "Pay with Crypto" crypto payment solution, currently primarily targeting the US iGaming and Daily Fantasy sports markets. The solution allows users to top up their accounts using stablecoins and major cryptocurrencies. Data shows that approximately 70.4 million US adults hold cryptocurrency, and 83% of gamers have expressed interest in crypto payments. Paysafe users can complete payments by connecting their crypto wallets or scanning a QR code; the system will automatically convert fiat currency upon transaction confirmation. (Businesswire)

data.title: The Ministry of State Security warns of the risks of "word-based scams": Claims of "getting rich quick by hoarding words" are suspected of being illegal financial activities and could endanger national economic security.
data.description: An article published on the official WeChat account of the Department of Homeland Security points out that "word-based scams" are emerging one after another, especially those involving "getting rich quick by hoarding words" and "profiting from over-the-counter trading," which are suspected of being illegal financial activities. These scams could be exploited by foreign intelligence agencies to endanger national economic security. While embracing and utilizing new technologies and applications, it is crucial to guard against risks and ensure security, including risks of data leaks and hijacking, forgery and tampering, and fraud traps. In the face of the word-based craze, it is essential to view its value rationally while paying attention to information security and privacy, raising awareness of security precautions, and understanding and using words wisely.

data.title: 360 recently discovered three vulnerabilities in OpenClaw: one high-risk and two medium-risk.
data.description: 360's vulnerability discovery AI recently uncovered and reported three high-value vulnerabilities in OpenClaw: one high-risk and two medium-risk. All newly discovered vulnerabilities have since been patched and publicly disclosed by 360. It is understood that these three newly discovered vulnerabilities directly target the core operating mechanism of the AI ​​agent, directly impacting the core security of user devices, data, and accounts. (The Paper)

data.title: U.S. Military Strikes Targets on Khark Island
data.description: The U.S. military has conducted strikes on military targets located on Khark Island, according to a report by AXIOS. According to BlockBeats, a U.S. official confirmed the operation. Further details about the strike and its implications have not been disclosed.

data.title: US strikes against Iranian targets exacerbate market turmoil; Iran is developing a plan to reopen its stock market.
data.description: A U.S. official stated that the U.S. military conducted strikes on military targets on Kharg Island. Meanwhile, Russia's crude oil prices have risen to their highest level in over 13 years, benefiting from the global oil price surge triggered by the situation in Iran. According to Argus Media, on April 2nd, at Primorsk, Russia's largest oil export facility on the Baltic coast, the price of the country's flagship Urals crude reached $116.05 per barrel. This price, excluding transportation costs, is almost double the average of $59 per barrel assumed in Russia's budget this year. Amid the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict, substantial oil revenues are easing the Kremlin's financial pressure. Furthermore, the head of Iran's Securities and Exchange Organization stated that four plans are currently being developed for the reopening of the Iranian stock market: 1. Maintaining existing conditions, i.e., allowing only fund trading, or reopening under existing conditions without public disclosure; 2. Escalating conflict, potentially leading to a suspension of all trading, including funds; 3. Reopening after a written ceasefire agreement; 4. Gradual reopening in the event of an unresolved ceasefire. The Iranian stock market was previously suspended on March 1st. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Bitcoin ETFs See $471M Inflows, Highest Since February as BTC Holds Below $70K
data.description: Key TakeawaysSpot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $471 million inflows on April 6, the highest since Feb. 25.BlackRock IBIT led with $182M, followed by Fidelity FBTC at $147M.ETF demand continues to offset weak spot buying and whale distribution.Markets price a ~98% probability of no Fed rate change, limiting near-term macro catalysts.Bitcoin ETF Inflows Hit Multi-Week HighU.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs posted $471 million in net inflows on April 6, marking the strongest daily intake in over a month and the sixth-largest inflow day of 2026.The surge comes as Bitcoin trades near $68,000–$69,000, continuing to face resistance below the $70,000 level.Institutional Demand Offsets Weak Market StructureETF inflows are increasingly acting as the primary source of marginal demand in the Bitcoin market.Despite ongoing selling from large holders and muted spot demand, institutional buying has helped stabilize price action, keeping Bitcoin range-bound rather than trending lower.Leading contributors to inflows included:BlackRock IBIT: $182MFidelity FBTC: $147MARK 21Shares (ARKB): $119MTotal ETF assets under management have recovered to above $90 billion, reflecting sustained institutional participation.Macro Conditions Provide Limited DirectionThe broader macro environment remains neutral.Prediction market data suggests a 98% probability that the Federal Reserve will hold interest rates steady, reducing expectations for near-term liquidity shifts.At the same time, geopolitical risks and inflation concerns continue to cap upside, contributing to a consolidation phase in Bitcoin.Bitcoin’s Role in Macro Markets Is EvolvingAccording to Binance Research, Bitcoin may be transitioning from a lagging to a leading macro asset.Historically, Bitcoin reacted to central bank policy changes. However, ETF-driven institutional flows are now positioning ahead of expected monetary shifts, suggesting Bitcoin may increasingly price in future liquidity conditions before traditional markets.Ethereum ETFs Show Early Signs of RecoverySpot Ether ETFs also recorded $120 million in inflows, reversing recent outflows.However, Ethereum funds remain under pressure on a multi-month basis, highlighting a divergence in institutional preference between Bitcoin and other crypto assets.ETF Flows Remain Key DriverBitcoin’s near-term trajectory remains closely tied to ETF activity.Key factors to monitor include:Continuation of ETF inflows versus renewed outflowsOngoing whale distributionFederal Reserve policy signalsGeopolitical developmentsAs long as institutional demand persists, Bitcoin may remain supported despite weak underlying market breadth, with price action likely to stay range-bound below $70,000 until a stronger catalyst emerges.

data.title: Wintermute Weekly Report: Middle East geopolitics dominates markets, crypto market sentiment is subdued.
data.description: According to Wintermute's latest weekly report, the global market is entirely driven by Middle East geopolitics. Following Iran's ceasefire signal, US stocks and crude oil prices fell, while Trump's tough stance caused WTI crude oil to surge 11% to above $111. The 10-year US Treasury yield rose to 4.36%, with the market pricing in a zero probability of a Fed rate cut in April. This week's PCE data is under intense scrutiny. Crypto market sentiment is weak, with Bitcoin's weekly gain only 2% and the Fear & Greed Index at 9 (extreme fear). ETFs saw a net inflow of $1.32 billion in March, but turned to net outflows at the end of the month, with institutions switching from buying to selling. Ethereum performed relatively strongly, rising 4.2%, while Solana fell below $80 after a $285 million theft occurred due to the Drift protocol. Wintermute points out that Tuesday is a critical juncture in the conflict, with negotiations underway for a 45-day ceasefire framework. An escalation of the situation would further increase the risk premium for oil prices.

data.title: Nate Silver Criticizes X for Alleged Bias Towards Right-Leaning Influencers
data.description: Nate Silver has accused X of showing favoritism towards right-leaning influencers, citing Cluvio analytics that reported Elon Musk's engagement on the platform reaching 251 million year-to-date. According to NS3.AI, Nikita Bier countered Silver's claim, arguing that the example was flawed as the New York Times post was behind a paywall, accessible to only 0.1% of users.

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Decouples From Tech Stocks as Iran War and AI Pressures Reshape Market Dynamics
data.description: Key TakeawaysBitcoin gained 5%+ since Feb. 28, while iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF fell over 2%.Correlation dropped from ~1.0 to 0.13, signaling near-complete decoupling.BTC increasingly trades as a macro/geopolitical asset, not a tech proxy.AI concerns weigh on software stocks, adding divergence pressure.Bitcoin Breaks Correlation With Software StocksBitcoin is diverging from software equities after months of tight correlation, reflecting a shift in how investors are positioning across asset classes.Since the Iran conflict began on Feb. 28, Bitcoin has risen more than 5% and traded back above $69,000, while the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF—a key proxy for software stocks—has declined by over 2%.This marks a clear short-term divergence after both assets moved closely together throughout much of late 2025 and early 2026.Correlation Collapses as Market Regime ShiftsCorrelation between Bitcoin and IGV dropped sharply from nearly 1.0 in early February to 0.13 following the outbreak of the conflict, indicating near-total decoupling.The metric has since partially recovered to around 0.7, but remains below prior levels, suggesting a structural shift in market behavior.Historically, Bitcoin has often traded in line with high-growth tech stocks due to its risk-on characteristics. The recent breakdown signals a potential transition toward a different role.Bitcoin Trades as Macro Asset Amid Geopolitical RiskThe divergence appears driven by Bitcoin’s increasing sensitivity to macro and geopolitical factors.Amid the Iran conflict and rising oil prices, Bitcoin has behaved more like a macro hedge, benefiting from uncertainty and shifting capital flows.This contrasts with equities, which remain more directly impacted by earnings expectations and sector-specific risks.AI Concerns Weigh on Software StocksSoftware equities have come under pressure due to concerns around artificial intelligence.Major holdings within IGV—including Microsoft, Oracle, and Salesforce—face potential margin compression as AI lowers barriers to entry and increases competition.Investors are reassessing valuations across the software sector, particularly in Software-as-a-Service (SaaS), contributing to underperformance.Divergence Highlights Changing Market NarrativeWhile both Bitcoin and software stocks remain down year-to-date, their recent divergence highlights a shift in market narrative:Bitcoin: increasingly driven by macro liquidity and geopoliticsSoftware stocks: driven by earnings, valuation, and AI disruptionThis separation suggests Bitcoin may be transitioning away from being viewed solely as a high-beta tech asset.Structural Shift or Temporary Divergence?The key question for markets is whether this decoupling will persist.If geopolitical tensions remain elevated and macro conditions dominate, Bitcoin could continue to trade independently of equities.However, a return to risk-on conditions or easing macro pressures could re-align correlations.For now, Bitcoin’s divergence from tech stocks signals a potential regime shift, with implications for portfolio allocation and cross-asset positioning.

data.title: Peter Schiff Predicts Bitcoin's Future Performance
data.description: Peter Schiff has stated that if Bitcoin reaches $10,000 by the end of 2026, it will still be the best-performing asset of the next decade. According to Odaily, Schiff expressed confidence that Michael Saylor will use this potential to continue driving up Bitcoin's price and issue more stock to purchase Bitcoin. However, a 92% decline would make it the worst-performing investment for most long-term holders.

data.title: Fox News Integrates Kalshi Prediction Data Across Platforms
data.description: Fox News has announced the integration of Kalshi prediction data across its various platforms, according to NS3.AI. This collaboration aims to enhance the network's offerings by providing audiences with insights derived from Kalshi's prediction market data. Walter Bloomberg reported the development, highlighting the potential for enriched content and analysis for viewers.

data.title: Peter Schiff: Even if Bitcoin falls to $10,000 by the end of 2026, it will still be the best-performing asset of the next decade.
data.description: Peter Schiff wrote that if Bitcoin reaches $10,000 by the end of 2026, it will still be the best-performing asset of the next decade. I'm sure Michael Saylor will rely on this to continue pushing up the price of Bitcoin and issue more shares to buy it. But a 92% drop would make it the worst-performing investment for most long-term holders.

data.title: Avalanche Drops 9.7% Amid Market Correction and Geopolitical Tensions
data.description: Avalanche (AVAX) experienced a 9.7% decline in the past 24 hours, following gains from the previous day. According to NS3.AI, this downturn is attributed to a market-wide correction and geopolitical uncertainties surrounding the US-Iran conflict. AVAX is encountering resistance near the $10 mark and may find it challenging to rally unless broader market conditions improve.

data.title: Bank of England Deputy Governor Calls for Greater Understanding of Household Inflation Perceptions
data.description: The Bank of England's Deputy Governor, Lombardelli, emphasized the need for a deeper understanding of how households perceive inflation. According to Jin10, Lombardelli highlighted the importance of gauging public sentiment to better inform monetary policy decisions. This approach aims to address the challenges posed by inflationary pressures on household finances and ensure that policy measures are effectively tailored to mitigate these impacts.

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Analyst Highlights Weak Dollar Response to Energy Price Surge
data.description: Mitsubishi UFJ Bank's foreign exchange analyst, Derek Halpenny, noted in a report that the U.S. dollar's response to the recent surge in energy prices has been relatively mild. According to Jin10, Halpenny highlighted that compared to the inflation shock of 2022, the current economic foundation of the United States appears weaker. Since the onset of the conflict, crude oil prices have risen by nearly 60%, yet the dollar index has only strengthened by about 2.5%. This reaction from the foreign exchange market is more subdued than past data might suggest. Despite the United States being a net exporter of oil, the current price shock could have a more significant impact on the U.S. economy than anticipated, potentially affecting business confidence and employment.
